blurb: ARKit enables augmented reality experiences using the device camera, motion sensors, and LiDAR scanner, with privacy protections enforced at the framework level. Camera access requires explicit user authorization through TCC, and raw camera frames are processed by a system-managed ARKit service rather than being directly exposed to third-party app code. World map data, scene geometry, and tracked feature points are processed and retained on-device with no automatic transmission to Apple or third-party servers. The face tracking API for TrueDepth camera data carries additional entitlement requirements, restricting access to approved use cases and preventing misuse for covert biometric identification.
